title: Rail and rolling stock builders and repairers
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Design, develop, and evaluate the operation of marine vessels, ship machinery, and related equipment, such as power supply and propulsion systems. (Based on 27 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct pressure tests on vessels, such as boilers, and examine boilers, pressure vessels, tanks, or vats to locate defects, such as leaks, weak spots, or defective sections, so that they can be repaired.

Install manholes, handholes, taps, tubes, valves, gauges, or feedwater connections in drums of water tube boilers, using hand tools, and maintain equipment, making repairs or modifications when necessary.

Lay out plate, sheet steel, or other heavy metal and locate and mark bending and cutting lines, using protractors, compasses, and drawing instruments or templates; fabricate or alter parts at construction sites, using shears, hammers, punches, or drills; and shape metal material over anvils, blocks, or other forms, using hand tools.

Bell, bead with power hammers, or weld pressure vessel tube ends to ensure leakproof joints, and fasten seams or joints together with welds, bolts, cement, rivets, solder, caulks, metal drive clips, or bonds to assemble components into products or to repair sheet metal items.

Repair or replace defective pressure vessel parts, such as safety valves or regulators, using torches, jacks, caulking hammers, power saws, threading dies, welding equipment, or metalworking machinery, and bolt or arc weld pressure vessel structures and parts together, using wrenches or welding equipment.

Inspect assembled vessels or individual components, such as tubes, fittings, valves, controls, or auxiliary mechanisms, to locate any defects, and inspect or test parts to determine nature or cause of defects or malfunctions.

Transport prefabricated parts to construction sites for assembly and installation, and trim, file, grind, deburr, buff, or smooth surfaces, seams, or joints of assembled parts, using hand tools or portable power tools.

Maneuver completed roofing units into position for installation, and install assemblies, such as flashing, pipes, tubes, heating and air conditioning ducts, furnace casings, rain gutters, or downspouts in supportive frameworks.

Determine project requirements, such as scope, assembly sequences, or required methods or materials, using blueprints, drawings, or written or verbal instructions, and read and interpret blueprints, schematics, work specifications, drawings, or charts.

Shape seams, joints, or irregular edges of pressure vessel sections or structural parts to attain specified fit of parts, using cutting torches, hammers, files, or metalworking machines; straighten or reshape bent pressure vessel plates or structure parts, using hammers, jacks, or torches.

Conduct failure analyses, document results, and recommend corrective actions; test performance of vehicles that use alternative fuels or components that have been redesigned to increase fuel efficiency; and apply knowledge of engineering technology to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery.

Inspect, diagnose, maintain, and operate test setups and equipment to detect malfunctions; adjust, repair, or replace faulty components of test setups and equipment; and maintain service records of robotic equipment or automated production systems.

Install new robotic systems in stationary positions or on tracks, install equipment such as instrumentation and test equipment, and modify, maintain, or repair electrical, electronic, or mechanical components, equipment, or systems to ensure proper functioning.
Document robotics test procedures and results, and prepare written documentation of electromechanical test results.

Fabricate ducts for high efficiency heating, ventilating, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems to maximize efficiency of systems; verify that HVAC systems are designed, installed, and calibrated according to green certification standards; and perform building commissioning activities.

Record production, repair, and maintenance information for machines and equipment. Read work orders and specifications to determine machines and equipment requiring repair or maintenance. Set up and operate machines, adjusting controls to regulate operations, and collaborate with other workers to repair or move machinery. Inventory and requisition machine parts, transport machine parts and tools using cranes or hoists, and collect and discard worn parts to maintain work areas. Clean machinery using solvents and tools, and replace or repair materials in machines or equipment compartments. Conduct preventative maintenance, repair, and lubricate machines and equipment, including electrical systems, and maintain parts inventory. Diagnose malfunctions, assemble, disassemble, and inspect machinery or equipment, record conditions and repairs, perform scheduled maintenance, and verify repairs by operating machinery. Maintain and repair industrial processing machinery, and examine for damage or wear. Repair or replace broken components, clean, lubricate, and adjust parts as needed, using hand tools and specialized equipment. Test machines or heavy equipment for operability and efficiency, and analyze test results to diagnose problems. Maintain detailed records of repairs and maintenance. Train others in technical procedures as necessary.
Conduct analyses of ships, including stability, structural, weight, and vibration analyses, and evaluate operation of marine equipment during acceptance testing and shakedown cruises.

Establish arrangement of boiler room equipment and propulsion machinery, heating and ventilating systems, refrigeration equipment, piping, and other functional equipment, and maintain contact with contractors and clients to ensure completion of work at minimum cost.
Check, test, and maintain automatic controls and alarm systems, while coordinating activities with regulatory bodies to ensure repairs and alterations meet safety standards.
Investigate and observe tests on machinery and equipment for compliance with standards and conduct environmental, operational, or performance tests on marine machinery and equipment.

Prepare technical reports for engineering, management, or sales personnel, and assist drafters in developing the structural design of products using drafting tools or computer-assisted drafting equipment.
Maintain and coordinate repair of marine machinery and equipment for installation on vessels, and procure materials needed to repair marine equipment and machinery.
Design and oversee testing, installation, and repair of marine apparatus and equipment, and formulate conceptual designs of aeronautical or aerospace products or systems.

Conduct analytical, environmental, operational, or performance studies to develop designs for products like marine engines and equipment, and analyze data to determine the feasibility of product proposals.

Evaluate product data or designs from inspections or reports for conformance to engineering principles, customer requirements, and environmental regulations, and direct or coordinate activities of engineering or technical personnel involved in designing, fabricating, or testing marine products.
Develop specifications for vehicles powered by alternative fuels or alternative power methods and create design alternatives for vehicle components to increase fuel efficiency.
Maintain records of engineering department activities, including expenses and details of equipment maintenance and repairs, and schedule machine overhauls as well as the servicing of various systems.

Design structures for crop storage, animal shelter, and loading as well as providing advice on water quality and pollution management, while discussing plans with clients and other engineers for necessary evaluations.
Conduct tests on agricultural machinery and equipment to ensure adequate performance and assist engineers in developing new products or processes.
Conduct research studies that develop new concepts in automotive engineering or analyze project requests to determine feasibility and production time of products.
Conduct or direct system-level automotive testing and design control systems or algorithms related to automotive energy management or emissions management.
Inspect marine equipment and machinery to draw up work requests and prepare product or system layouts and detailed drawings and schematics.
Plan and direct construction of rural electric-power distribution systems, and develop or implement operating methods or procedures for engineering projects.
Conduct personnel functions, such as supervision of production workers, technicians, or other engineers, and provide technical direction related to the development or production of systems.
Review work requests and compare them with previous work completed on ships to ensure economically sound costs, whilst validating design of fuel cells or related systems.
Write performance requirements for engineering projects and prepare or present technical or project status reports.
Discuss, with clients, the needs that can improve agricultural productivity and conduct educational programs to provide information to farmers.
Explore and implement green technologies, including those related to agricultural machinery and implement applicable engineering techniques.
Enhance production activities by coordinating with other functional units such as procurement, maintenance, or quality control.
Analyze fuel cell test data and conduct research related to emerging fields of engineering, while developing or testing models of designs.
Implement changes to product designs to optimize performance and ensure safety in operation-related tasks.
Attend training sessions or conferences to stay informed about new tools or methods that may apply to engineering practices.
Conduct post-service analyses using diagnostic principles or procedures to facilitate ongoing performance assessments.
Monitor inventory levels, order supplies as necessary, and monitor tool and part inventories to ensure adequate working conditions.
Assist scientists or engineers in the conduct of photonic experiments and compute or record photonic test data.
Set up or operate assembly or processing equipment, such as lasers, cameras, or die bonders, and adjust or maintain equipment including lasers or microscopes.
Design, build, or modify fixtures used to assemble parts and lay out cutting lines for machining using drafting tools.
Assemble or adjust parts of prototypes, and build prototype optomechanical devices for use in equipment.
Splice fibers using fusion splicing techniques, and terminate, cure, polish, or test fiber cables with mechanical connectors.
Examine structures or vehicles such as aircraft or trains using non-destructive testing techniques, and visually examine materials for signs of flaws.
Conduct liquid penetrant tests and interpret results according to applicable codes, standards, and specifications.
Document non-destructive testing methods and develop new testing methods such as thermal or infrared testing.
Inspect and monitor work areas, provide employee safety training, and conduct or arrange for worker training in safety and operational procedures.
Compute estimates and actual costs of materials or labor, and participate in budget preparation and administration.
Determine schedules, sequences, and assignments for work activities, based on work priority and personnel skills, while monitoring employees' work levels.
Investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports of findings, and confer with personnel to coordinate work activities and resolve grievances.
Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations using hand or power tools, and document procedures such as the calibration of optical equipment.
Develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures, and interpret specifications or blueprints to lay out reference points.

emerging_tasks.task: Operate, test, and troubleshoot uncrewed aerial systems, commonly known as drones, to ensure optimal performance.
Program and calibrate drones for specific missions or tasks, ensuring proper functionality and performance.
Inventory and store parts, tools, and equipment.
Troubleshoot electrical, hydraulic, or mechanical equipment and machines.
Communicate results in peer-reviewed research articles or at workshops or conferences.
Develop and test autonomous systems for uncrewed aerospace vehicles.
Develop plans for safely drydocking vessels, including structural integrity, block loading, and stability.
Investigate equipment failures or diagnose faulty operations and recommend or perform remedial actions.
Use agricultural drones for crop monitoring, irrigation management, and pest control.
Operate drones for remote inspection of large or hard-to-reach structures, such as wind turbines, bridges, or tall buildings.
